theme: Sawmill with one dying thin blade
title: Sawmill with One Dying Thin Blade
seed_problem: guillotine cutting-stock with kerf, valued rectangles
mutation: composite: guillotine cut-tree output annotated with a per-cut blade choice, coupled to a global thin-blade wear (cut-length) budget and a kerf-consumption ledger; the objective becomes value extracted under a resource that the recursion itself spends, turning packing feasibility into a function of the cut-tree shape.
strategies: trivial: cut only the wide base of every shelf with the reliable thick blade (reproduces the checker baseline)
naive greedy: spend the thin-blade budget on the highest-VALUE bonus pieces first (buys tall premium shelves, blows the budget, starves the many cheap shelves)
cut-order greedy: use the thin blade on the first/longest structural cuts encountered top-down (exhausts L on long cuts that unlock nothing)

insight (strong): ration the wear budget by VALUE PER UNIT thin cut-length (density / fractional-knapsack), filling up on efficient shelves; a general solver can go further by mixing blades within a shelf
